 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-pm5.3","title":"Onboarding: Auto-run Doctor After Hook Installation","description":"## Problem
After installing the hook, users don't know if everything is working. They discover issues only when builds are slow (fell back to local). The first-run experience should validate the entire setup.

## Solution
Auto-run abbreviated doctor after `rch hook install`:
```
$ rch hook install

✓ Hook installed to Claude Code

Running quick health check...
  ✓ Daemon running (PID 12345)
  ✓ 2 workers healthy
  ✓ SSH connectivity verified
  ⚠ Worker 'gpu-1' has circuit breaker open

Setup complete! Your next cargo build will compile remotely.
Issues found: 1 warning (run 'rch doctor' for details)
```

## Implementation Details
- After successful hook install, run doctor checks
- Use abbreviated check set (skip slow network tests)
- Summarize results: passed, warnings, failures
- If failures: suggest `rch doctor` for full report
- If warnings: show inline summary

## Abbreviated Checks
1. Daemon running
2. At least one healthy worker
3. SSH keys accessible
4. Hook status verified

## Full Doctor Checks (for reference)
- Prerequisites (rsync, zstd, ssh)
- Config file validity
- SSH key permissions
- All worker connectivity
- Toolchain alignment

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/commands.rs - modify hook install to call doctor
- rch/src/doctor.rs - add quick_check mode

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Doctor runs automatically after hook install
- [ ] Quick checks complete in <5 seconds
- [ ] Clear summary of setup health
- [ ] Warnings shown inline
- [ ] Failures prompt full doctor run

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-pm5.4","title":"Onboarding: Show Success Message on First Remote Build","description":"## Problem
The hook is intentionally silent. But for first-time users, this creates uncertainty. "Did it actually work? Was that build remote or local?" The first successful remote build should provide confirmation.

## Solution
Track first successful build and show one-time message:
```
$ cargo build
   Compiling myproject v0.1.0
    Finished release [optimized] target(s) in 2.3s

──────────────────────────────────────────────────
🎉 First remote build complete!

Your build ran on 'css' in 2.3s (local would be ~7.5s)
RCH is now working silently in the background.

To see build activity: rch status --jobs
To disable this message: rch config set first_run_complete true
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

## Implementation Details
- Store `first_run_complete: false` in config
- After first successful remote build, show message
- Set `first_run_complete: true` to suppress future messages
- Estimate local time from historical data or benchmarks

## Message Content
- Confirm build ran remotely
- Show actual vs estimated local time
- Explain silent operation going forward
- Suggest status command for future visibility

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/config.rs - add first_run_complete field
- rch/src/hook.rs - check flag, show message, set flag

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Message shows only once per installation
- [ ] Shows worker name and timing
- [ ] Can be disabled via config
- [ ] Message goes to stderr
- [ ] Doesn't break output parsing

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-pm5.5","title":"Onboarding: SSH Setup Guidance in Error Messages","description":"## Problem
SSH errors are the #1 blocker for new users. Error messages say "Permission denied (publickey)" but don't explain:
- How to generate SSH keys
- How to copy keys to workers
- How to use SSH agent
- How to debug SSH issues

## Solution
Enhance SSH-related errors with contextual guidance:
```
$ rch workers probe --all

Worker: gpu-1
  Error: SSH connection failed - Permission denied (publickey)

SSH Troubleshooting:
  1. Verify key exists:
     ls -la ~/.ssh/id_rsa

  2. Copy key to worker:
     ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/id_rsa ubuntu@gpu-1

  3. Test connection manually:
     ssh -i ~/.ssh/id_rsa ubuntu@gpu-1 echo "success"

  4. If using SSH agent:
     eval $(ssh-agent) && ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_rsa

Run 'rch doctor' for comprehensive SSH diagnostics.
```

## Implementation Details
- Detect SSH error type (permission, timeout, host key)
- Provide specific guidance for each error type
- Include actual paths from config
- Suggest debug command with -v flag

## Error Types and Guidance
- Permission denied: key copy instructions
- Connection refused: check host/port
- Connection timeout: check network/firewall
- Host key verification: known_hosts instructions
- Agent forwarding: eval ssh-agent instructions

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/error.rs - add SSH error variants with help
- rch/src/commands.rs - use typed errors in probe
- rch/src/doctor.rs - expand SSH checks with guidance

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SSH errors include troubleshooting steps
- [ ] Steps use actual config values (paths, hosts)
- [ ] Different guidance for different error types
- [ ] Debug command provided
- [ ] Doctor reference for more help
","notes":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_ssh_permission_denied_includes_guidance() {
    info\!("TEST START: test_ssh_permission_denied_includes_guidance");
    let err = SshError::PermissionDenied { 
        host: "gpu-1".into(),
        user: "ubuntu".into(),
        key_path: PathBuf::from("/home/user/.ssh/id_rsa"),
    };
    info\!("INPUT: SshError::PermissionDenied for ubuntu@gpu-1");
    let msg = format\!("{}", err);
    info\!("RESULT: Error message:\n{}", msg);
    assert\!(msg.contains("ssh-copy-id"));
    assert\!(msg.contains("ubuntu@gpu-1"));
    assert\!(msg.contains("/home/user/.ssh/id_rsa"));
    info\!("VERIFY: Message includes ssh-copy-id command with actual host/path");
    info\!("TEST PASS: test_ssh_permission_denied_includes_guidance");
}

#[test]
fn test_ssh_timeout_includes_guidance() {
    info\!("TEST START: test_ssh_timeout_includes_guidance");
    let err = SshError::ConnectionTimeout {
        host: "10.0.0.5".into(),
        timeout_secs: 30,
    };
    info\!("INPUT: SshError::ConnectionTimeout for 10.0.0.5");
    let msg = format\!("{}", err);
    info\!("RESULT: Error message:\n{}", msg);
    assert\!(msg.contains("firewall") || msg.contains("network"));
    assert\!(msg.contains("10.0.0.5"));
    info\!("VERIFY: Message includes network troubleshooting guidance");
    info\!("TEST PASS: test_ssh_timeout_includes_guidance");
}

#[test]
fn test_ssh_host_key_includes_guidance() {
    info\!("TEST START: test_ssh_host_key_includes_guidance");
    let err = SshError::HostKeyVerificationFailed { host: "gpu-1".into() };
    info\!("INPUT: SshError::HostKeyVerificationFailed");
    let msg = format\!("{}", err);
    info\!("RESULT: Error message:\n{}", msg);
    assert\!(msg.contains("known_hosts") || msg.contains("ssh-keyscan"));
    info\!("VERIFY: Message includes known_hosts troubleshooting");
    info\!("TEST PASS: test_ssh_host_key_includes_guidance");
}
```

### Integration Tests
```bash
# Test with unreachable host
$ rch workers probe worker-that-does-not-exist 2>&1 | grep -q "Troubleshooting"

# Verify doctor reference included
$ rch workers probe bad-worker 2>&1 | grep -q "rch doctor"
```

All tests must log:
- The specific SSH error type
- The generated error message
- Which troubleshooting guidance is included","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:00.544597898-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T18:00:58.010327746-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T18:00:58.010327746-05:00","close_reason":"Added SSH error guidance usage in benchmarks/discovery + improved doctor/key path display","labels":["errors","onboarding","ssh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-pm5.5","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-pm5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:00.581148126-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-pm5.6","title":"Onboarding: Clear Daemon Lifecycle Documentation and Guidance","description":"## Problem
There are 4 ways to start the daemon:
1. `rchd` (foreground)
2. `rch daemon start` (background)
3. systemd service
4. launchd plist

Users don't know which to use or how they differ. The current documentation is scattered and incomplete.

## Solution
Add clear guidance for daemon lifecycle:
```
$ rch daemon --help

The RCH daemon must be running for remote compilation to work.

Quick Start:
  rch daemon start      Start in background (recommended for daily use)
  rch daemon stop       Stop background daemon
  rch daemon status     Check if running

For Development:
  rchd                  Run in foreground (logs to terminal)
  rchd --verbose        Run with debug logging

For Production:
  sudo systemctl enable rch    Linux: auto-start on boot
  brew services start rch      macOS: auto-start on login

Current Status:
  Daemon: Running (PID 12345)
  Uptime: 2h 15m
  Socket: /tmp/rch.sock
```

## Implementation Details
- Enhance `rch daemon --help` with guidance
- Add `rch daemon status` that shows current state
- Detect and suggest appropriate method for platform
- Warn if daemon not running in relevant commands

## Platform-Specific Guidance
- Linux: systemctl commands
- macOS: launchctl or brew services
- WSL: Background process or Windows service

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/main.rs - enhance daemon subcommand help
- rch/src/commands.rs - add daemon status improvements
- docs/guides/daemon.md - comprehensive documentation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --help explains all options clearly
- [ ] status shows complete daemon state
- [ ] Platform-appropriate suggestions
- [ ] Warns when daemon not running
- [ ] Auto-start instructions documented

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-q3u","title":"Task: Unit Tests for Telemetry Collection","description":"## Overview
Implement unit tests for telemetry collection functions with comprehensive structured logging, specifically the /proc filesystem parsing and metric calculation logic.

## Background and Justification
Telemetry collection reads from /proc (Linux) and calculates derived metrics (CPU %, memory pressure, I/O rates). These calculations must be correct, or worker load information will be inaccurate.

## Logging Infrastructure

### Test Logging Setup
```rust
// rch-telemetry/tests/common/mod.rs
use tracing::{info, debug, warn, error, instrument, span, Level};
use tracing_subscriber::{fmt, EnvFilter, layer::SubscriberExt, util::SubscriberInitExt};
use std::sync::Once;

static INIT: Once = Once::new();

pub fn init_test_logging() {
    INIT.call_once(|| {
        let filter = EnvFilter::try_from_default_env()
            .unwrap_or_else(|_| EnvFilter::new("debug"));
        
        tracing_subscriber::registry()
            .with(fmt::layer()
                .with_test_writer()
                .with_target(true)
                .with_file(true)
                .with_line_number(true)
                .with_thread_ids(true)
                .json())
            .with(filter)
            .init();
    });
}

/// Macro for consistent test logging
#[macro_export]
macro_rules! test_log {
    ($phase:expr, $($field:tt)*) => {
        info!(
            test_module = module_path!(),
            test_phase = $phase,
            $($field)*
        )
    };
}
```

## Test Categories with Detailed Logging

### 1. /proc Parsing Tests
```rust
// rch-telemetry/src/collect/cpu_tests.rs

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use crate::tests::common::init_test_logging;
    use tracing::{info, debug, instrument};
    use tracing_test::traced_test;
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_parse_proc_stat() {
        info!(
            test = "test_parse_proc_stat",
            phase = "setup",
            description = "Testing /proc/stat CPU line parsing"
        );
        
        let sample = r#"cpu  10132153 290696 3084719 46828483 16683 0 25195 0 0 0
cpu0 1393280 32966 572056 13343292 6130 0 17875 0 0 0"#;
        
        debug!(
            test = "test_parse_proc_stat",
            phase = "input",
            sample_lines = 2,
            sample_preview = &sample[..50]
        );
        
        info!(test = "test_parse_proc_stat", phase = "execute");
        let result = parse_proc_stat(sample);
        
        debug!(
            test = "test_parse_proc_stat",
            phase = "result",
            is_ok = result.is_ok()
        );
        
        let stats = result.expect("parsing should succeed");
        
        info!(
            test = "test_parse_proc_stat",
            phase = "assert",
            expected_user = 10132153,
            actual_user = stats.total_user,
            expected_system = 3084719,
            actual_system = stats.total_system,
            expected_idle = 46828483,
            actual_idle = stats.total_idle
        );
        
        assert_eq!(stats.total_user, 10132153, "user time mismatch");
        assert_eq!(stats.total_system, 3084719, "system time mismatch");
        assert_eq!(stats.total_idle, 46828483, "idle time mismatch");
        
        info!(
            test = "test_parse_proc_stat",
            phase = "complete",
            status = "PASSED"
        );
    }
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_parse_proc_stat_malformed_input() {
        info!(
            test = "test_parse_proc_stat_malformed_input",
            phase = "setup",
            description = "Testing graceful handling of malformed /proc/stat"
        );
        
        let malformed_samples = vec![
            ("empty", ""),
            ("no_cpu_line", "other stuff"),
            ("truncated", "cpu  10132153"),
            ("non_numeric", "cpu  abc def ghi jkl"),
            ("negative", "cpu  -100 -200 -300 -400"),
        ];
        
        for (name, sample) in malformed_samples {
            debug!(
                test = "test_parse_proc_stat_malformed_input",
                case = name,
                sample_len = sample.len()
            );
            
            let result = parse_proc_stat(sample);
            
            info!(
                test = "test_parse_proc_stat_malformed_input",
                case = name,
                is_err = result.is_err(),
                error_msg = result.as_ref().err().map(|e| e.to_string())
            );
            
            assert!(result.is_err(), "case '{}' should fail", name);
        }
        
        info!(
            test = "test_parse_proc_stat_malformed_input",
            phase = "complete",
            status = "PASSED",
            cases_tested = malformed_samples.len()
        );
    }
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_parse_proc_meminfo() {
        info!(
            test = "test_parse_proc_meminfo",
            phase = "setup",
            description = "Testing /proc/meminfo parsing"
        );
        
        let sample = r#"MemTotal:       16384000 kB
MemFree:         8192000 kB
MemAvailable:   10240000 kB
Buffers:          512000 kB
Cached:          2048000 kB
SwapTotal:       4096000 kB
SwapFree:        4096000 kB"#;
        
        debug!(
            test = "test_parse_proc_meminfo",
            phase = "input",
            fields = vec!["MemTotal", "MemFree", "MemAvailable", "Buffers", "Cached"]
        );
        
        let mem = parse_proc_meminfo(sample).expect("parsing should succeed");
        
        info!(
            test = "test_parse_proc_meminfo",
            phase = "assert",
            total_kb = mem.total_kb,
            available_kb = mem.available_kb,
            used_pct = format!("{:.1}%", 100.0 * (1.0 - mem.available_kb as f64 / mem.total_kb as f64))
        );
        
        assert_eq!(mem.total_kb, 16384000);
        assert_eq!(mem.available_kb, 10240000);
        
        info!(test = "test_parse_proc_meminfo", phase = "complete", status = "PASSED");
    }
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_parse_proc_diskstats() {
        info!(
            test = "test_parse_proc_diskstats",
            phase = "setup",
            description = "Testing /proc/diskstats parsing for disk I/O"
        );
        
        let sample = r#"   8       0 sda 12345 6789 1000000 50000 5432 2100 500000 25000 0 30000 75000
   8       1 sda1 10000 5000 800000 40000 4000 1800 400000 20000 0 25000 60000
 253       0 nvme0n1 50000 0 2000000 100000 30000 0 1500000 80000 0 120000 180000"#;
        
        debug!(
            test = "test_parse_proc_diskstats",
            phase = "input",
            devices = vec!["sda", "sda1", "nvme0n1"]
        );
        
        let disks = parse_proc_diskstats(sample).expect("parsing should succeed");
        
        info!(
            test = "test_parse_proc_diskstats",
            phase = "assert",
            sda_reads = disks.get("sda").map(|d| d.reads_completed),
            sda_sectors_read = disks.get("sda").map(|d| d.sectors_read),
            nvme_reads = disks.get("nvme0n1").map(|d| d.reads_completed)
        );
        
        assert_eq!(disks.get("sda").unwrap().reads_completed, 12345);
        assert_eq!(disks.get("sda").unwrap().sectors_read, 1000000);
        assert_eq!(disks.get("nvme0n1").unwrap().reads_completed, 50000);
        
        info!(test = "test_parse_proc_diskstats", phase = "complete", status = "PASSED");
    }
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_parse_proc_net_dev() {
        info!(
            test = "test_parse_proc_net_dev",
            phase = "setup",
            description = "Testing /proc/net/dev parsing for network I/O"
        );
        
        let sample = r#"Inter-|   Receive                                                |  Transmit
 face |bytes    packets errs drop fifo frame compressed multicast|bytes    packets errs drop fifo colls carrier compressed
    lo: 1234567    12345    0    0    0     0          0         0  1234567    12345    0    0    0     0       0          0
  eth0: 98765432  987654    0    0    0     0          0         0 45678901   456789    0    0    0     0       0          0
 bond0: 198765432 1987654   5    2    0     0          0         0 95678901   956789    0    0    0     0       0          0"#;
        
        let net = parse_proc_net_dev(sample).expect("parsing should succeed");
        
        info!(
            test = "test_parse_proc_net_dev",
            phase = "assert",
            interfaces_found = net.interfaces.len(),
            eth0_rx_bytes = net.interfaces.get("eth0").map(|i| i.rx_bytes),
            eth0_tx_bytes = net.interfaces.get("eth0").map(|i| i.tx_bytes)
        );
        
        assert_eq!(net.interfaces["eth0"].rx_bytes, 98765432);
        assert_eq!(net.interfaces["eth0"].tx_bytes, 45678901);
        assert!(net.interfaces.contains_key("lo"));
        assert!(net.interfaces.contains_key("bond0"));
        
        info!(test = "test_parse_proc_net_dev", phase = "complete", status = "PASSED");
    }
}
```

### 2. Metric Calculation Tests with Logging
```rust
// rch-telemetry/src/collect/metrics_tests.rs

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use tracing::{info, debug};
    use tracing_test::traced_test;
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_cpu_percentage_calculation() {
        info!(
            test = "test_cpu_percentage_calculation",
            phase = "setup",
            description = "Verifying CPU percentage calculated from delta"
        );
        
        let prev = CpuStats { 
            user: 1000, 
            nice: 0,
            system: 500, 
            idle: 8500, 
            iowait: 0,
            irq: 0,
            softirq: 0,
        };
        let curr = CpuStats { 
            user: 1100, 
            nice: 0,
            system: 550, 
            idle: 8850, 
            iowait: 0,
            irq: 0,
            softirq: 0,
        };
        
        debug!(
            test = "test_cpu_percentage_calculation",
            prev_total = 1000 + 500 + 8500,
            curr_total = 1100 + 550 + 8850,
            delta_total = 500,
            delta_active = 150
        );
        
        let pct = calculate_cpu_percent(&prev, &curr);
        
        info!(
            test = "test_cpu_percentage_calculation",
            phase = "assert",
            expected = "30% (150/500)",
            actual = format!("{:.1}%", pct),
            tolerance = 0.1
        );
        
        assert!(
            (pct - 30.0).abs() < 0.1,
            "CPU percentage should be ~30%, got {:.1}%",
            pct
        );
        
        info!(test = "test_cpu_percentage_calculation", phase = "complete", status = "PASSED");
    }
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_memory_pressure_calculation() {
        info!(
            test = "test_memory_pressure_calculation",
            phase = "setup"
        );
        
        let mem = MemoryInfo { 
            total_kb: 16_000_000, 
            available_kb: 4_000_000,
            free_kb: 2_000_000,
            buffers_kb: 500_000,
            cached_kb: 1_500_000,
        };
        
        let pressure = calculate_memory_pressure(&mem);
        
        info!(
            test = "test_memory_pressure_calculation",
            phase = "assert",
            total_gb = 16.0,
            available_gb = 4.0,
            used_pct = 75.0,
            calculated_pressure = pressure
        );
        
        assert!((pressure - 75.0).abs() < 0.1);
        
        info!(test = "test_memory_pressure_calculation", phase = "complete", status = "PASSED");
    }
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_disk_io_rate_calculation() {
        info!(
            test = "test_disk_io_rate_calculation",
            phase = "setup"
        );
        
        let t0 = Instant::now();
        let prev = DiskStats { 
            sectors_read: 1_000_000, 
            sectors_written: 500_000,
            timestamp: t0,
        };
        let curr = DiskStats { 
            sectors_read: 1_100_000, 
            sectors_written: 550_000,
            timestamp: t0 + Duration::from_secs(1),
        };
        
        debug!(
            test = "test_disk_io_rate_calculation",
            delta_sectors = 100_000,
            delta_time_secs = 1,
            sector_size_bytes = 512
        );
        
        let rate_mbps = calculate_disk_read_rate(&prev, &curr);
        
        info!(
            test = "test_disk_io_rate_calculation",
            phase = "assert",
            expected_mbps = 51.2,
            actual_mbps = rate_mbps,
            calculation = "100000 * 512 / 1 / 1024 / 1024"
        );
        
        assert!((rate_mbps - 51.2).abs() < 0.1);
        
        info!(test = "test_disk_io_rate_calculation", phase = "complete", status = "PASSED");
    }
}
```

### 3. Edge Case Tests with Logging
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod edge_case_tests {
    use super::*;
    use tracing::{info, warn, debug};
    use tracing_test::traced_test;
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_zero_time_delta() {
        info!(
            test = "test_zero_time_delta",
            phase = "setup",
            description = "Testing graceful handling of zero time delta"
        );
        
        let stats = CpuStats::default();
        
        warn!(
            test = "test_zero_time_delta",
            phase = "execute",
            warning = "Zero time delta - should return 0.0 without panic"
        );
        
        let rate = calculate_cpu_percent(&stats, &stats);
        
        info!(
            test = "test_zero_time_delta",
            phase = "assert",
            result = rate,
            expected = 0.0
        );
        
        assert_eq!(rate, 0.0);
        
        info!(test = "test_zero_time_delta", phase = "complete", status = "PASSED");
    }
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_counter_overflow() {
        info!(
            test = "test_counter_overflow",
            phase = "setup",
            description = "Testing u64 counter overflow handling"
        );
        
        let prev = CpuStats { 
            user: u64::MAX - 100,
            system: 0,
            idle: 0,
            ..Default::default()
        };
        let curr = CpuStats { 
            user: 50,  // Wrapped around
            system: 0,
            idle: 0,
            ..Default::default()
        };
        
        warn!(
            test = "test_counter_overflow",
            phase = "execute",
            prev_user = u64::MAX - 100,
            curr_user = 50,
            note = "Counter wrapped from near-max to small value"
        );
        
        let pct = calculate_cpu_percent(&prev, &curr);
        
        info!(
            test = "test_counter_overflow",
            phase = "assert",
            result = pct,
            in_valid_range = pct >= 0.0 && pct <= 100.0
        );
        
        assert!(pct >= 0.0 && pct <= 100.0, "Result should be in valid range");
        
        info!(test = "test_counter_overflow", phase = "complete", status = "PASSED");
    }
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_missing_network_interface() {
        info!(
            test = "test_missing_network_interface",
            phase = "setup",
            description = "Testing interface that disappeared between samples"
        );
        
        let mut prev_interfaces = HashMap::new();
        prev_interfaces.insert("eth0".to_string(), InterfaceStats::default());
        prev_interfaces.insert("eth1".to_string(), InterfaceStats::default());
        
        let mut curr_interfaces = HashMap::new();
        curr_interfaces.insert("eth0".to_string(), InterfaceStats::default());
        // eth1 missing!
        
        warn!(
            test = "test_missing_network_interface",
            phase = "execute",
            prev_interfaces = vec!["eth0", "eth1"],
            curr_interfaces = vec!["eth0"],
            missing = vec!["eth1"]
        );
        
        let prev = NetworkStats { interfaces: prev_interfaces };
        let curr = NetworkStats { interfaces: curr_interfaces };
        
        let rate = calculate_network_rate(&prev, &curr);
        
        info!(
            test = "test_missing_network_interface",
            phase = "assert",
            eth0_present = rate.contains_key("eth0"),
            eth1_present = rate.contains_key("eth1")
        );
        
        assert!(rate.contains_key("eth0"));
        assert!(!rate.contains_key("eth1"));  // Should be absent, not panic
        
        info!(test = "test_missing_network_interface", phase = "complete", status = "PASSED");
    }
}
```

## Test Data Fixtures
Create fixtures at `rch-telemetry/tests/fixtures/`:
- `proc_stat_sample.txt` - Representative /proc/stat output
- `proc_stat_idle.txt` - System at idle
- `proc_stat_busy.txt` - System under load
- `proc_meminfo_sample.txt` - Normal memory state
- `proc_meminfo_low.txt` - Low memory condition
- `proc_diskstats_sample.txt` - Multiple disks
- `proc_net_dev_sample.txt` - Multiple interfaces

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-qgs","title":"Add build history tracking to daemon","description":"## Overview

Add build history tracking in the daemon. This should record the most recent builds (success/failure, durations, worker, project), enabling `rch status`, TUI, and the web dashboard.

## Goals

1. In-memory ring buffer of recent builds (default 100)
2. Record start + end timestamps
3. Store exit code, worker, project, command
4. Optionally persist to disk for daemon restart survival

## Data Model

```rust
// rchd/src/history/mod.rs

use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use std::collections::VecDeque;
use std::sync::RwLock;

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct BuildRecord {
    /// Unique build identifier
    pub id: u64,
    /// When the build started
    pub started_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    /// When the build completed
    pub completed_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    /// Project identifier
    pub project_id: String,
    /// Worker that executed the build (None if local)
    pub worker_id: Option<String>,
    /// Full command executed
    pub command: String,
    /// Exit code (0 = success)
    pub exit_code: i32,
    /// Duration in milliseconds
    pub duration_ms: u64,
    /// Build location
    pub location: BuildLocation,
    /// Bytes transferred (if remote)
    pub bytes_transferred: Option<u64>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum BuildLocation {
    Local,
    Remote,
}

pub struct BuildHistory {
    /// Ring buffer of recent builds
    records: RwLock<VecDeque<BuildRecord>>,
    /// Maximum capacity
    capacity: usize,
    /// Next build ID
    next_id: AtomicU64,
    /// Persistence path (optional)
    persistence_path: Option<PathBuf>,
}

impl BuildHistory {
    pub fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            records: RwLock::new(VecDeque::with_capacity(capacity)),
            capacity,
            next_id: AtomicU64::new(1),
            persistence_path: None,
        }
    }

    pub fn with_persistence(mut self, path: PathBuf) -> Self {
        self.persistence_path = Some(path);
        self
    }

    /// Record a completed build
    pub fn record(&self, record: BuildRecord) {
        let mut records = self.records.write().unwrap();
        if records.len() >= self.capacity {
            records.pop_front();
        }
        records.push_back(record);

        // Optionally persist
        if let Some(ref path) = self.persistence_path {
            let _ = self.persist_async(path);
        }
    }

    /// Get recent builds (most recent first)
    pub fn recent(&self, limit: usize) -> Vec<BuildRecord> {
        let records = self.records.read().unwrap();
        records.iter().rev().take(limit).cloned().collect()
    }

    /// Get builds by worker
    pub fn by_worker(&self, worker_id: &str, limit: usize) -> Vec<BuildRecord> {
        let records = self.records.read().unwrap();
        records.iter()
            .rev()
            .filter(|r| r.worker_id.as_deref() == Some(worker_id))
            .take(limit)
            .cloned()
            .collect()
    }

    /// Get statistics
    pub fn stats(&self) -> BuildStats {
        let records = self.records.read().unwrap();
        let total = records.len();
        let successes = records.iter().filter(|r| r.exit_code == 0).count();
        let remote = records.iter().filter(|r| r.location == BuildLocation::Remote).count();
        let avg_duration = if total > 0 {
            records.iter().map(|r| r.duration_ms).sum::<u64>() / total as u64
        } else {
            0
        };

        BuildStats {
            total_builds: total,
            success_count: successes,
            failure_count: total - successes,
            remote_count: remote,
            local_count: total - remote,
            avg_duration_ms: avg_duration,
        }
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct BuildStats {
    pub total_builds: usize,
    pub success_count: usize,
    pub failure_count: usize,
    pub remote_count: usize,
    pub local_count: usize,
    pub avg_duration_ms: u64,
}
```

## Implementation

### Recording Hooks

```rust
// rchd/src/executor.rs

impl BuildExecutor {
    pub async fn execute(&self, request: BuildRequest) -> Result<BuildResult> {
        let build_id = self.history.next_id();
        let started_at = Utc::now();

        let result = self.do_execute(request.clone()).await;

        let completed_at = Utc::now();
        let duration_ms = (completed_at - started_at).num_milliseconds() as u64;

        // Record to history
        self.history.record(BuildRecord {
            id: build_id,
            started_at,
            completed_at,
            project_id: request.project_id,
            worker_id: result.as_ref().ok().and_then(|r| r.worker_id.clone()),
            command: request.command,
            exit_code: result.as_ref().map(|r| r.exit_code).unwrap_or(-1),
            duration_ms,
            location: if result.as_ref().map(|r| r.is_remote).unwrap_or(false) {
                BuildLocation::Remote
            } else {
                BuildLocation::Local
            },
            bytes_transferred: result.as_ref().ok().and_then(|r| r.bytes_transferred),
        });

        result
    }
}
```

### Persistence

```rust
// rchd/src/history/persistence.rs

impl BuildHistory {
    /// Load history from JSONL file
    pub fn load_from_file(path: &Path, capacity: usize) -> Result<Self> {
        let file = File::open(path)?;
        let reader = BufReader::new(file);

        let mut records = VecDeque::with_capacity(capacity);
        let mut max_id = 0u64;

        for line in reader.lines() {
            let record: BuildRecord = serde_json::from_str(&line?)?;
            max_id = max_id.max(record.id);
            if records.len() >= capacity {
                records.pop_front();
            }
            records.push_back(record);
        }

        Ok(Self {
            records: RwLock::new(records),
            capacity,
            next_id: AtomicU64::new(max_id + 1),
            persistence_path: Some(path.to_path_buf()),
        })
    }

    /// Persist to JSONL file (append mode)
    fn persist_record(&self, path: &Path, record: &BuildRecord) -> Result<()> {
        let mut file = OpenOptions::new()
            .create(true)
            .append(true)
            .open(path)?;

        writeln!(file, "{}", serde_json::to_string(record)?)?;
        Ok(())
    }

    /// Compact the persistence file (keep only capacity records)
    pub fn compact(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
        let records = self.records.read().unwrap();
        let temp_path = path.with_extension("tmp");

        let mut file = File::create(&temp_path)?;
        for record in records.iter() {
            writeln!(file, "{}", serde_json::to_string(record)?)?;
        }

        std::fs::rename(temp_path, path)?;
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (rchd/src/history/tests.rs)

```rust
#[test]
fn test_ring_buffer_capacity() {
    let history = BuildHistory::new(3);

    for i in 0..5 {
        history.record(make_build_record(i));
    }

    let recent = history.recent(10);
    assert_eq!(recent.len(), 3); // Capped at capacity
    assert_eq!(recent[0].id, 5); // Most recent first
    assert_eq!(recent[2].id, 3); // Oldest retained
}

#[test]
fn test_recent_ordering() {
    let history = BuildHistory::new(10);
    history.record(make_build_record(1));
    history.record(make_build_record(2));
    history.record(make_build_record(3));

    let recent = history.recent(2);
    assert_eq!(recent.len(), 2);
    assert_eq!(recent[0].id, 3); // Most recent first
    assert_eq!(recent[1].id, 2);
}

#[test]
fn test_by_worker_filter() {
    let history = BuildHistory::new(10);
    history.record(BuildRecord {
        worker_id: Some("worker-1".to_string()),
        ..make_build_record(1)
    });
    history.record(BuildRecord {
        worker_id: Some("worker-2".to_string()),
        ..make_build_record(2)
    });
    history.record(BuildRecord {
        worker_id: Some("worker-1".to_string()),
        ..make_build_record(3)
    });

    let worker1_builds = history.by_worker("worker-1", 10);
    assert_eq!(worker1_builds.len(), 2);
    assert!(worker1_builds.iter().all(|b| b.worker_id.as_deref() == Some("worker-1")));
}

#[test]
fn test_stats_calculation() {
    let history = BuildHistory::new(10);

    // 2 successes, 1 failure, 2 remote, 1 local
    history.record(BuildRecord {
        exit_code: 0,
        location: BuildLocation::Remote,
        duration_ms: 1000,
        ..make_build_record(1)
    });
    history.record(BuildRecord {
        exit_code: 0,
        location: BuildLocation::Remote,
        duration_ms: 2000,
        ..make_build_record(2)
    });
    history.record(BuildRecord {
        exit_code: 1,
        location: BuildLocation::Local,
        duration_ms: 500,
        ..make_build_record(3)
    });

    let stats = history.stats();
    assert_eq!(stats.total_builds, 3);
    assert_eq!(stats.success_count, 2);
    assert_eq!(stats.failure_count, 1);
    assert_eq!(stats.remote_count, 2);
    assert_eq!(stats.local_count, 1);
    assert_eq!(stats.avg_duration_ms, 1166); // (1000+2000+500)/3
}

#[test]
fn test_empty_history() {
    let history = BuildHistory::new(10);

    assert!(history.recent(10).is_empty());
    assert!(history.by_worker("any", 10).is_empty());

    let stats = history.stats();
    assert_eq!(stats.total_builds, 0);
    assert_eq!(stats.avg_duration_ms, 0);
}

#[test]
fn test_thread_safety() {
    use std::thread;

    let history = Arc::new(BuildHistory::new(100));

    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..10)
        .map(|i| {
            let h = Arc::clone(&history);
            thread::spawn(move || {
                for j in 0..10 {
                    h.record(make_build_record(i * 10 + j));
                }
            })
        })
        .collect();

    for handle in handles {
        handle.join().unwrap();
    }

    let recent = history.recent(200);
    assert_eq!(recent.len(), 100); // All 100 recorded
}

fn make_build_record(id: u64) -> BuildRecord {
    BuildRecord {
        id,
        started_at: Utc::now(),
        completed_at: Utc::now(),
        project_id: "test-project".to_string(),
        worker_id: None,
        command: "cargo build".to_string(),
        exit_code: 0,
        duration_ms: 100,
        location: BuildLocation::Local,
        bytes_transferred: None,
    }
}
```

### Persistence Tests (rchd/src/history/persistence_test.rs)

```rust
#[test]
fn test_persistence_save_load() {
    let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let path = tmp.path().join("history.jsonl");

    // Create and populate history
    let history = BuildHistory::new(5).with_persistence(path.clone());
    for i in 1..=3 {
        history.record(make_build_record(i));
    }

    // Load into new instance
    let loaded = BuildHistory::load_from_file(&path, 5).unwrap();
    let recent = loaded.recent(10);

    assert_eq!(recent.len(), 3);
    assert_eq!(recent[0].id, 3);
}

#[test]
fn test_persistence_append_mode() {
    let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let path = tmp.path().join("history.jsonl");

    // First session
    {
        let history = BuildHistory::new(10).with_persistence(path.clone());
        history.record(make_build_record(1));
        history.record(make_build_record(2));
    }

    // Second session
    {
        let history = BuildHistory::load_from_file(&path, 10).unwrap();
        history.record(make_build_record(3));
    }

    // Third session - verify all records
    let history = BuildHistory::load_from_file(&path, 10).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(history.recent(10).len(), 3);
}

#[test]
fn test_compaction() {
    let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let path = tmp.path().join("history.jsonl");

    // Create history with 3 records but capacity 2
    let history = BuildHistory::new(2).with_persistence(path.clone());
    for i in 1..=3 {
        history.record(make_build_record(i));
    }

    // Compact
    history.compact(&path).unwrap();

    // Verify file only has 2 records
    let loaded = BuildHistory::load_from_file(&path, 10).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(loaded.recent(10).len(), 2);
}

#[test]
fn test_corrupt_file_handling() {
    let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let path = tmp.path().join("history.jsonl");

    // Write some valid records + garbage
    std::fs::write(&path, r#"{"id":1,"started_at":"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z","completed_at":"2024-01-01T00:00:01Z","project_id":"test","worker_id":null,"command":"test","exit_code":0,"duration_ms":1000,"location":"Local","bytes_transferred":null}
not valid json
"#).unwrap();

    // Should handle gracefully (skip bad lines or error)
    let result = BuildHistory::load_from_file(&path, 10);
    // Implementation can either skip bad lines or return error
    // Both are acceptable behaviors
}
```

```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_history_recorded_on_build() {
    let daemon = TestDaemon::start().await;

    // Execute a build
    let result = daemon.client.build("cargo build").await.unwrap();

    // Check history
    let status = daemon.client.status().await.unwrap();
    assert!(!status.recent_builds.is_empty());
    assert_eq!(status.recent_builds[0].command, "cargo build");
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_history_survives_restart() {
    let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let config = DaemonConfig {
        history_path: Some(tmp.path().join("history.jsonl")),
        ..Default::default()
    };

    // First daemon instance
    {
        let daemon = TestDaemon::start_with_config(config.clone()).await;
        daemon.client.build("cargo build").await.unwrap();
    }

    // Second daemon instance
    {
        let daemon = TestDaemon::start_with_config(config).await;
        let status = daemon.client.status().await.unwrap();
        assert_eq!(status.recent_builds.len(), 1);
    }
}

    for i in 0..5 {
        daemon.client.build(&format!("cargo build {}", i)).await.ok();
    }

    let status = daemon.client.status().await.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(status.recent_builds.len(), 5);

    // Verify ordering (most recent first)
    assert!(status.recent_builds[0].command.contains("4"));
}
```

### E2E Test Script (scripts/e2e_history_test.sh)

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
RCH="${RCH:-$SCRIPT_DIR/../target/release/rch}"
RCHD="${RCHD:-$SCRIPT_DIR/../target/release/rchd}"
TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
LOG_FILE="$TEST_DIR/e2e_history.log"
DAEMON_PID=""

export RCH_MOCK_SSH=1
export RCH_LOG_LEVEL=debug
export RCH_SOCKET="$TEST_DIR/rch.sock"
export RCH_DATA_DIR="$TEST_DIR/data"

log() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }
pass() { log "PASS: $1"; }
fail() { log "FAIL: $1"; cleanup; exit 1; }

cleanup() {
    if [[ -n "$DAEMON_PID" ]]; then
        kill "$DAEMON_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

mkdir -p "$RCH_DATA_DIR"

log "=== RCH Build History E2E Test ==="
log "Test dir: $TEST_DIR"

# Start daemon
start_daemon() {
    log "Starting daemon..."
    "$RCHD" --socket "$RCH_SOCKET" --data-dir "$RCH_DATA_DIR" &
    DAEMON_PID=$!
    sleep 2

    if ! kill -0 "$DAEMON_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
        fail "Daemon failed to start"
    fi
}

stop_daemon() {
    if [[ -n "$DAEMON_PID" ]]; then
        kill "$DAEMON_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
        wait "$DAEMON_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
        DAEMON_PID=""
    fi
}

# Test 1: History starts empty
test_empty_history() {
    log "Test 1: Empty history"

    OUTPUT=$("$RCH" status --json 2>&1)
    BUILDS=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(len(json.load(sys.stdin).get('recent_builds', [])))")

    log "  Initial builds: $BUILDS"
    [[ "$BUILDS" == "0" ]] || fail "History should start empty"
    pass "Empty history"
}

# Test 2: Builds are recorded
test_build_recording() {
    log "Test 2: Build recording"

    # Simulate some builds (may need to use actual build or mock)
    for i in 1 2 3; do
        # This depends on having a working mock build path
        "$RCH" build --dry-run "cargo build $i" 2>&1 || true
    done

    OUTPUT=$("$RCH" status --json 2>&1)
    BUILDS=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(len(json.load(sys.stdin).get('recent_builds', [])))" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    log "  Recorded builds: $BUILDS"
    pass "Build recording"
}

# Test 3: History limit respected
test_history_limit() {
    log "Test 3: History limit"

    # Record many builds
    for i in $(seq 1 150); do
        "$RCH" build --dry-run "test $i" 2>&1 >/dev/null || true
    done

    OUTPUT=$("$RCH" status --json 2>&1)
    BUILDS=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(len(json.load(sys.stdin).get('recent_builds', [])))" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    log "  History size: $BUILDS (should be <= 100)"
    [[ "$BUILDS" -le 100 ]] || log "  Warning: history may exceed limit"
    pass "History limit"
}

# Test 4: History persistence across restart
test_persistence() {
    log "Test 4: Persistence across restart"

    # Check current count
    OUTPUT=$("$RCH" status --json 2>&1)
    BEFORE=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(len(json.load(sys.stdin).get('recent_builds', [])))" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    log "  Before restart: $BEFORE builds"

    # Restart daemon
    stop_daemon
    sleep 1
    start_daemon

    OUTPUT=$("$RCH" status --json 2>&1)
    AFTER=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(len(json.load(sys.stdin).get('recent_builds', [])))" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    log "  After restart: $AFTER builds"

    [[ "$AFTER" == "$BEFORE" ]] || log "  Note: counts differ (may indicate persistence not enabled)"
    pass "Persistence"
}

# Test 5: History file format
test_file_format() {
    log "Test 5: History file format"

    HISTORY_FILE="$RCH_DATA_DIR/build_history.jsonl"
    if [[ -f "$HISTORY_FILE" ]]; then
        log "  History file exists: $HISTORY_FILE"
        log "  First 3 lines:"
        head -3 "$HISTORY_FILE" | while read -r line; do
            log "    $line"
            echo "$line" | python3 -c "import json,sys; json.load(sys.stdin)" || log "    (invalid JSON)"
        done
    else
        log "  Note: history file not found (may use different path)"
    fi
    pass "File format"
}

# Test 6: Stats calculation
test_stats() {
    log "Test 6: Build stats"

    OUTPUT=$("$RCH" status --json 2>&1)
    log "  Stats from status:"
    echo "$OUTPUT" | python3 -c "
import json, sys
d = json.load(sys.stdin)
if 'daemon' in d:
    print('    builds_today:', d.get('daemon', {}).get('builds_today', 'N/A'))
if 'stats' in d:
    print('    stats:', d.get('stats'))
" 2>/dev/null || log "  (unable to parse stats)"

    pass "Stats"
}

# Run tests
start_daemon
test_empty_history
test_build_recording
test_history_limit
test_persistence
test_file_format
test_stats

log "=== All History E2E tests passed ==="
log "Full log at: $LOG_FILE"
cat "$LOG_FILE"
```

- `/status` API (remote_compilation_helper-3sy)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T12:15:56.044171161-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T23:24:17.640843684-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T23:24:17.640843684-05:00","close_reason":"BuildHistory and /status API fully implemented and tested - all 349 tests pass"}
 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-qir","title":"Build Queue Visibility and Status","description":"## Problem
Users have no visibility into:
- What builds are currently running on which workers
- What builds are queued waiting for workers
- Estimated wait time when all workers are busy
- Why their build hasnt started yet

This is especially frustrating with multiple concurrent projects.

## Solution
Add build queue visibility to both CLI and web dashboard:

### CLI: rch queue
```
$ rch queue
ACTIVE BUILDS
  ID        PROJECT           WORKER        STARTED    ELAPSED
  b-a1b2    myproject         gpu-server-1  10:32:15   2m 15s
  b-c3d4    otherproject      cpu-server-2  10:33:01   1m 29s

QUEUED (2 waiting)
  ID        PROJECT           QUEUED AT     WAIT TIME
  b-e5f6    thirdproject      10:34:00      ~30s (1 worker freeing soon)
  b-g7h8    fourthproject     10:34:05      ~2m (behind thirdproject)

Workers: 2/3 busy, 1 offline
```

### CLI: rch status --watch
Real-time build status updates:
```
$ rch status --watch
Watching build status... (Ctrl+C to exit)

[10:34:30] b-a1b2 (myproject) completed on gpu-server-1 (2m 45s)
[10:34:31] b-e5f6 (thirdproject) started on gpu-server-1
[10:35:15] b-c3d4 (otherproject) completed on cpu-server-2 (2m 14s)
[10:35:16] b-g7h8 (fourthproject) started on cpu-server-2
```

### Web Dashboard: Queue Panel
- Real-time updating queue display
- Progress bars for active builds
- Estimated completion times
- Worker assignment visualization

## Implementation Details

### Queue Data Structure
```rust
pub struct BuildQueue {
    pub active: Vec<ActiveBuild>,
    pub queued: VecDeque<QueuedBuild>,
}

pub struct ActiveBuild {
    pub id: BuildId,
    pub project_id: String,
    pub worker_id: WorkerId,
    pub started_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub command: String,
}

pub struct QueuedBuild {
    pub id: BuildId,
    pub project_id: String,
    pub queued_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub estimated_start: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
}
```

### API Endpoints
- `GET /api/queue` - Current queue state
- `WS /api/queue/stream` - Real-time queue updates

### Estimated Wait Time Calculation
```rust
fn estimate_wait_time(queue: &BuildQueue, position: usize) -> Duration {
    // Sum expected remaining time of builds ahead in queue
    let mut wait = Duration::ZERO;
    
    // Time until next worker frees up
    let next_free = queue.active.iter()
        .map(|b| b.estimated_remaining())
        .min();
    
    // Add time for queued builds ahead of this one
    for i in 0..position {
        wait += queue.queued[i].estimated_duration;
    }
    
    wait
}
```

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_wait_time_estimation() {
    info\!("TEST START: test_wait_time_estimation");
    let queue = make_test_queue(active: 2, queued: 3);
    info\!("INPUT: Queue with 2 active, 3 queued builds");
    
    let wait = estimate_wait_time(&queue, 2);
    info\!("RESULT: Estimated wait for position 2: {:?}", wait);
    
    assert\!(wait > Duration::ZERO);
    info\!("TEST PASS: test_wait_time_estimation");
}
```

### E2E Tests
- Queue displays correctly with multiple builds
- Wait time updates as builds complete
- WebSocket stream delivers real-time updates
